title: Announcing the 2025
description: We're a fast-moving DoD organization contracting with commercial companies to solve national security problems
text:
WASHINGTON, D.C. (September 24, 2025) — The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) recognizes the selection of 10 contracting officers for the fourth cohort of the Immersive Commercial Acquisition Program (ICAP) for 2025-2026.
The ICAP Fellowship, a collaboration between the Defense Acquisition University (DAU) and DIU, offers an immersive 12-month fellowship that provides hands-on experience in commercial acquisition. The selected fellows for the flagship innovation program are:
Danielle Beavers-Rose, U.S. Coast Guard
Patrick Conrad, Air Force
Ryan Cunard, Army
Stephen Johnson, Defense Contracting Management Agency
Lacey Lockard, Defense Health Agency
Nichelle Morris, Department of the Navy
Kelsie O’Connell, Navy
Dyanne Van Der Kamp, Navy
Chelsie Voss, Air Force
Jennifer Weyand, Defense Health Agency
Launched in May 2022, ICAP fellowship is a unique opportunity for acquisition professionals to work directly with cutting-edge technology companies, learn from industry experts, and gain valuable experience in commercial acquisition. Fellows benefit from an immersive experience that combines DAU learning with relevant, on-the-job training through the execution of DIU prototype projects.
ICAP is a critical component of the Department of Defense's efforts to stay ahead of the curve in the rapidly changing technological landscape. The program provides acquisition professionals with the knowledge, skills, and experience needed to navigate the complex world of commercial acquisition.
To celebrate the accomplishments of the outgoing 2024-25 cohort and welcome the new fellows, DIU and DAU will host an ICAP Recognition Ceremony at Howell Auditorial at the Defense Acquisition University campus on Ft. Belvoir, Virginia on January 28, 2026.
